description: Legal document: partnership agreement. Dated: July 18, 1107. Location: Cairo. Partnership agreement between Nethanel b. Tiqva and Eli b. Mevasser in a clothing and tailoring shop. Nethanel and Eli will both sell the clothing, which Eli will sew. Nethanel contributes 10 dinars, expecting to contribute more once the shop is in operation, and is the shop keyholder. He will receive two-thirds of the profits and losses; Eli is to receive the balance. Lines 1 and 4 mention God’s role in guiding the partnership and in bringing about profit, which is common in such documents. Four diagonal lines run through the entire text; it is unclear whether this indicates that the partnership was terminated. The document is signed by Shelomo b. Mevorakh. (Information from Lieberman, "A Partnership Culture," 210)
description_he: מסמך משפטי: הסכם שותפות. תאריך: 18 ביולי 1107. מקום: קהיר. שותפות בין נתנאל ב' תקווה ועלי ב' מבשר בחנות בגדים ותפירה. נתנאל משקיע 10 דינרים ויגבה שני שלישים מהרווחים.
